Fix integration tests broken by logging consolidation

The logging consolidation in commit 66398ee moved 'Guards enforcement'
and 'Registered tool:' messages from stderr (log.Printf) to the file
logger (logger.LogInfo) and debug logger (logUnified.Printf). This
broke 4 integration tests that read these strings from stderr.

Changes:
- difc_config_test.go: Wait for 'Starting MCPG' (still on stderr) and
  assert '[DIFC] Registered guard noop' instead of 'Guards enforcement
  disabled' for all 3 DIFC tests
- playwright_test.go: Verify playwright server registration via the
  /health endpoint instead of grepping stderr for tool log messages

commit.message:
Fix E2E test failures: networkidle and SSR visibility

Accessibility tests: Replace page.waitForLoadState('networkidle') with
'domcontentloaded'. The block editor has continuous network activity
(heartbeat, autosave) that prevents networkidle from ever resolving,
causing test timeouts and browser crashes.

Documents shortcode test: Use toHaveCount(1) instead of toBeVisible().
ServerSideRender returns empty content when no documents exist,
producing a zero-height wrapper that Playwright considers "hidden"
even though the block is correctly inserted.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: uptime monitor — dual-schedule + 90s timeout + retry on cold-start

Problems with previous monitor:
- Single cron */5 can be delayed 10-30+ min by GitHub — Render spins down in 15min
- 45s max-time expired before Render cold-start finished (takes 50-90s)
- On 000 response, gave up with no retry

Fixes:
- TWO overlapping schedules: */5 (at :00,:05..) and 2-59/5 (at :02,:07..)
  Together fire every ~2-3 min — both would need delay simultaneously to miss
- 90s max-time handles full Render cold-start window
- On 000: wait 90s then ping again to confirm wake + reset idle timer
- On 503: wait 30s then ping again
- Single job pings both sequentially (saves GitHub Actions runner quota)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: live demo system + showcase demo with sample project

- New 'live' step type in demo runner — sends real prompts to Copilot,
  waits for idle between steps (with configurable timeout + pauseAfter)
- /api/demos endpoint lists available demos
- Mode button now opens a demo picker instead of hardcoding 'intro'
- demo/sample-app: tiny Node.js task tracker project for demos
  (store.js, format.js, index.js, tests/run.js — all working)
- demos/showcase.json: 6-step live demo showcasing explore, edit, test

commit.message:
test(core): VT500 state machine TABLE transition tests (43 tests) (#120)

Directly tests the transition TABLE in packages/core/src/parser/states.ts
covering all key state transitions:

- GROUND: printable ASCII, high bytes, C0 controls, DEL
- Anywhere rules: ESC (0x1b), CAN/SUB (0x18/0x1a), ST (0x9c), C1 controls
  from every state — including the DCS_PASSTHROUGH UNHOOK override for 0x9c
- ESCAPE: bracket/bracket-sequences, intermediates, final dispatch bytes
- CSI_ENTRY/CSI_PARAM: digit params, private markers, intermediates, finals
- OSC_STRING: BEL terminator, printable data bytes
- DCS_ENTRY/DCS_PASSTHROUGH: hook, param, PUT data, UNHOOK on ST
- CSI_IGNORE/SOS_PM_APC_STRING: absorb-until-final semantics

Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(concourse): cross-env GitHub issue gate for split Concourse pipelines

When a pipeline is split across two Concourse environments (e.g. QA stack in QA
Concourse, Production stack in prod Concourse) the normal intra-chain GitHub issue
promotion gate is broken: pulumi_jobs_chain only creates the trigger resource when
there is a next stage in the same chain.

Changes:
- Add prior_stage_stack field to SimplePulumiParams: when set, build_simple_pulumi_pipeline
  creates a github_issues trigger resource for that stack and injects it as a
  custom_dependencies[0] entry so the first job in the new chain gates on the prior
  stage's deployment issue being closed.
- Set prior_stage_stack="substructure.starrocks.lakehouse.QA" on the
  starrocks-substructure (Production-only) entry so the production deployment in prod
  Concourse waits for the QA deployment issue that was posted by QA Concourse.
- Fix mypy type annotation for extra_args in meta.py __main__ block.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix worker skill system: gain, display, output modifier, junction skip

Worker skill gain:
- Moved to top of production tick loop (before canProduce/delivery checks)
- Workers now gain 0.055 skill/day while building is active, even without inputs
- Rate: ~5 years from 0 to 100

Worker skill output modifier:
- Changed from 0.90+avg*0.01 to 1.0+totalSkill*0.0005
- Each worker at skill 100 = +5% output (no penalty for low skill)
- 3 workers at max = +15% total

UI display fixes:
- Building detail now shows person.workerSkill (was reading wrong skills object)
- Skill shown with 1 decimal place for growth visibility
- Output formula line includes skill modifier

Quality crafting:
- avgWorkerSkill now correctly sourced from workerSkill property
- Good: 30% + avgSkill*0.4% base, up to 90% with player skill
- Excellent: 10% + avgSkill*0.3% base, up to 60% with player skill

Road junctions:
- tickTownCategories() now skips junctions (was triggering village downgrade)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Kornia GPU augmentation backend for detection training (#874)

* feat: Kornia GPU augmentation backend for detection training
* fix: wire gpu_postprocess through build_o365_raw to prevent double normalize
* fix: add _kornia_setup_done sentinel; promote auto fallback log to WARNING
* fix: blur_limit min 3; docstrings for gpu_postprocess and unpack_boxes
* test: add coverage for blur_limit edge, o365 guard, sentinel re-run, pipeline forward pass
* fix: GPU path falls back to AUG_CONFIG not {} when aug_config=None
* fix: square gaussian kernel; device-move Kornia modules; pre-resolve auto backend
* fix: add missing kornia import in o365.py has_kornia check

feat: user preference for preserve-vs-overwrite embedded metadata/art (#342) (#343)

* feat(application): add EmbeddedTagPreference for preserve-vs-overwrite tag behavior

* fix: make overwrite_existing a computed property to enforce single source of truth

Replace the redundant `overwrite_existing: bool` field on `TagEmbeddingOptions`
with a computed method `overwrite_existing()` derived from `preference`.  This
eliminates any possibility of the two fields being set inconsistently (e.g. via
struct update syntax).  `preference` is now the sole source of truth.

Also update ROADMAP.md wording per reviewer suggestion: "no writes" → "do not
overwrite existing content".

Addresses review feedback on PR #343.

commit.message:
feat: add JavaScript and TypeScript hook executors (#7626)

* feat: add JavaScript and TypeScript hook executors

Implements JavaScript (Phase 2) and TypeScript (Phase 3) hook executors
for the multi-language hooks system. Both executors follow the
HookExecutor interface (Prepare/Execute/Cleanup) and reuse existing
node.Cli for package manager detection and dependency installation.

- JavaScript executor: auto-detects package.json, runs `node script.js`
- TypeScript executor: uses `npx tsx` for zero-config TS execution
- Shared Node.js helpers to keep JS/TS DRY
- IoC registration for "js" and "ts" hook kinds
- Unit and E2E tests for both executors
- Documentation updates for JS/TS hooks

Closes #7621
Closes #7622

commit.message:
fix: prevent zero-cost purchase — coupon CAS, min order amount, amount tolerance

- Coupon use_coupon() now has CAS guard: used_count < max_uses in the
  UPDATE WHERE clause, preventing concurrent over-use
- Coupon discount capped at 95% of subtotal (was 100%)
- Percentage coupon creation capped at 95% (was 100%)
- Minimum order amount enforced at ¥0.10 (was ¥0.01)
- Quantity must be >= 1 (explicit check)
- Coupon usage failure now propagated as error (was silently ignored)
- Payment callback amount tolerance changed from fixed ±0.01 to
  relative max(2%, ¥0.01) — prevents tiny-amount bypass

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf(maglev): replace CheckedSmi* with Generic* ops in graph builder

Replace all CheckedSmiAdd/Sub/Mul/Div/Mod/Inc/Dec with Generic* equivalents
and remove explicit CheckSmi guards from arithmetic and comparison emitters.

This fixes the #1 root cause of deopt-blocked benchmarks:
- Generic ops are arithmetic-compatible consumers, enabling smi_guarded
  analysis on upstream LoadNamedGeneric and LoadGlobal nodes
- smi_guarded loads get speculative Smi coercion at the load site
- Generic ops have runtime fallbacks (jit_runtime_generic_*) instead
  of permanent deopt on overflow
- StoreGlobal added to arithmetic-compatible consumer list

Expected impact:
- property_access_1k: 955µs → ~3µs (deopt blocked → Maglev runs)
- sieve_primes_1k: 2429µs → improved (deopt blocked → Maglev runs)
- All arithmetic benchmarks: no regression (smi_guarded path is same speed)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fontconfig gnome-49: add -Dcache-dir=/usr/lib/fontconfig/cache

Fixes Flatpak apps not discovering host fonts (GH#21).
Without this flag, fc-cache writes to /var/cache/fontconfig which
is lost during container builds. The persistent path
/usr/lib/fontconfig/cache is exposed to Flatpak sandboxes via
/run/host/fonts-cache/.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix duplicate models and Ollama 404 on chat

- ListModelsEndpoint: add DistinctBy(m => m.Name) to both the primary
  and fallback model lists to prevent duplicates when AllowedModels
  contains the same entry twice (e.g. via env var concatenation).
- OllamaHttpClient: add NormalizeModelName helper that appends ':latest'
  to untagged model names before calling /api/chat. Ollama's API returns
  404 for untagged names (e.g. 'llama3.1'); the full tag is required
  ('llama3.1:latest'). Log lines keep the original user-facing name.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Security hardening for Phase 4: Hash API keys, improve path validation, enhance command injection detection

- SECURITY-001: Implement Argon2/PBKDF2 hashing for API keys (was: plaintext)
  - Add _hash_key() and _verify_hashed_key() with constant-time comparison
  - Automatic migration of existing plaintext keys on startup
  - File permissions set to 0o600 (owner read/write only)
  - New dependency: argon2-cffi>=23.0.0

- SECURITY-002: Fix path traversal validation in terminal tool
  - Replace naive '..' splitting with os.path.realpath() resolution
  - Validate that resolved paths exist and are directories
  - Consistent with filesystem tools' path handling

- SECURITY-003: Enhance command injection prevention
  - Expand DANGEROUS_PATTERNS blocklist (11 patterns now)
  - Add netcat/bash pipe detection
  - Add /dev/zero and /dev/tcp detection
  - New STRICT_COMMAND_MODE env var for allowlist-only execution

- SECURITY-004: Add comprehensive security test suite
  - 4 path traversal prevention tests
  - 9 command injection prevention tests
  - 3 API key hashing tests
  - Total: 16 new security-focused tests

All changes are backward-compatible. Existing installations automatically
migrate plaintext keys to hashes on startup.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add C# debugging, MCP server, multi-language architecture

- C# debugging via netcoredbg: fully working (breakpoints, locals, eval, step)
- Multi-language launcher system: Python (debugpy), C# (netcoredbg),
  Java (java-debug) and Go (Delve) launcher stubs
- DAP client: now supports both TCP and pipe transports
- MCP server: JSON-RPC over stdio with full tool definitions
- Exception breakpoints + conditional breakpoints support
- C# sample app + demo script

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Modernize Docker configurations

Root docker-compose.yml:
- Upgrade MySQL 8.0 (EOL) to 8.4 LTS
- Add health check on db service
- Add depends_on with service_healthy condition for WordPress and phpMyAdmin
- Change restart policy from always to unless-stopped
- Upgrade phpMyAdmin to latest

Devcontainer Dockerfile:
- Consolidate RUN layers (5 to 4) for smaller image
- Remove redundant packages already in base image (git, less, unzip, wget)
- Add --no-install-recommends for smaller apt layer
- Validate WP-CLI phar after download

Devcontainer docker-compose.yml:
- Upgrade MariaDB from 10.11 to lts tag (currently 11.8)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: security round 4 — transaction safety, token-based auth, post-action reliability

A. Withdrawal transaction + CAS: wrap balance deduction + withdrawal insert
   in a single DB transaction with conditional UPDATE to prevent overdraft.

B. Order query_token: add per-order high-entropy token for public queries.
   Payment return redirect includes token for seamless auto-query.
   Email-based order listing removed; legacy email fallback only for
   pre-token orders.

C. AFF query by code: public AFF endpoints now use AFF code (16-char)
   instead of email. Prevents email enumeration.

D. Post-action status tracking: orders track post_action_status
   (pending/success/failed). Failed actions auto-retry up to 3 times
   via cleanup task. Orders don't reach false-positive delivered state.

E. Frontend super_admin guard: route-level meta + beforeEach check.
   403 responses handled distinctly from 401 (permission denied vs
   unauthorized).

F. Tests: 6 id_gen tests covering query_token generation, AFF code
   length, and uniqueness.

Files changed: 22 files across backend services, entities, migrations,
API routes, frontend views, router, i18n, and API client.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update Goods Guide: multi-recipe display, quality crafting info, quest category

- Recipe tracking now supports multiple recipes per good (e.g. rope from hemp OR cloth)
- Quality tier goods show crafting chances, skills, cascade, soldier effectiveness
- Basic tier goods note that good/excellent variants exist
- Added quest category to filter buttons and sort order
- Fixed alternative recipe producer/consumer tracking for all buildings

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix devcontainer OOM: defer WP test framework install

The postCreateCommand was OOM-killed (exit code 137) in Codespaces
because script/install-wp-tests downloads WordPress core via SVN,
which is memory-intensive when combined with the prior
updateContentCommand (composer, npm, webpack).

Changes:
- Remove set -e so individual step failures don't abort setup
- Defer WP test framework install to manual step
- Limit Node.js heap during block build (512 MB)
- Update README with manual test framework setup instructions
- Update WordPress image references to 6.9

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove cell/font size caps for proper HiDPI card readability

- Remove min(160) cap on cell size in _recompute_layout: cells now scale
  freely with physical surface dimensions (185px on 2x Retina vs 160 capped)
- Remove min(28) font size cap in _draw_card_on_cell: font now proportional
  to cell size without ceiling (font=37px on 2x Retina vs 26px before)
- Change font/band ratio from size//6 to size//5 for better text proportion
- On 2x Retina (3600x1800 physical): font goes from ~12pt to ~17pt → readable

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: Fix Settings screen dead buttons #383 (#403)

* docs(.squad): Merge audit sprint directives into decisions.md

- Audit Sprint Deferred Work Directive: #392–#394 moved to backlog per user request (scope control)
- Workout State Persistence Architecture: Room + Gson design decision for PR #399 (#390)
  - Single in_progress_workouts table with JSON serialization
  - Auto-save triggers on set completion, exercise addition, rest timer start
  - Graceful corruption handling (silent clear + log)
  - Rationale: zero data loss on app kill, fast recovery, existing dependencies

Merged from .squad/decisions/inbox/ — inbox files deleted.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove dead Sign In button and make App Version read-only

Removes the non-functional Sign In row and its dialog from SettingsScreen
since the auth backend is a NoOp stub. Makes the App Version row read-only
by removing its onClick handler and dialog. The SettingsRow composable now
accepts an optional onClick parameter - when null, the row is not clickable.

Closes #383

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add Stage 7 Ch.5: 计算与图像的统一

Add the final chapter of Stage 7 covering:
- KP1: Compute Shader, CUDA/OpenCL, Roofline model
- KP2: Ray Tracing hardware acceleration (RT Core, BVH, Möller-Trumbore)
- KP3: DLSS/FSR AI super-resolution and frame generation
- KP4: Unified Shader Architecture, Mesh Shaders, GPU-driven rendering

Includes 8 quiz questions, 4 SVG figures, 4 inline quizzes,
and 20 step-cards (5 per KP). Next nav is disabled (last chapter).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: JamesPAN-spst <228010148+JamesPAN-spst@users.noreply.github.com>
